reviews.review_description:
I don't usually write a review until I've 100% completed a game, but I'd say I'm about 2 thirds the way through Crashland and it's been TOUGH to get this far even on normal difficulty level.

Firstly, you will die a lot in this game on certain levels in particular. I noticed that some reviews have criticised the level of difficulty to the point of frustration and quitting. I personally like a challenge. The game is very simple on the surface. You have to survive for a set amount of minutes in each environment until you're rescued while you're attacked from all angles by an incredible variety of enemies. You get a pistol and boom stick. Your pistol has infinite primary ammo and a finite number of more powerful secondary fire rounds. Collectable power balls top up your secondary ammo charges. A bunch of power-ups are air dropped, so you can acquire a variety of different pistol fire modes and boom stick special weapons; this slowly recharges once unlocked. You get a limited number of teleports, which slowly recharge. These must be used wisely to get out of harms way.

As you progress and level up, you unlock perks that you pick and choose before beginning each mission. There's so much variety here; one of my personal favourite perks is the deployable auto cannon, which you can call for once each mission.

There is a lot of strategy here, and you have to think fast on occasions or find yourself completely overrun and very dead. Again, you will die A LOT. But, in doing so and if you don't give up like some of the other reviewers, you will learn how certain enemies behave and attack and how to counter.

The graphics are pretty solid despite the game being released in 2021, in the Quest 2 era. I'm playing on Q3, and I think there may have been some upgrades. There is a stupendous variety in the enemy types across the game; the devs really have come up trumps here. They look great and are animated well. They all behave differently. Some will charge you, some will grab you, some will blind you, launch projectiles, glue you to the spot, generate other enemies, or even eat your power balls and grow bigger. There's even a T Rex on one level! If you hate bugs and spiders, this game will test you to your absolute limit.

The devs apparently launched a major update to this game in Dec 2025, adding more enemies, special missions, and weapons. I'd give them extra kudos for that for still supporting the game so long after it's initial release. To me, it shows they value existing players and new players alike.

i really do love this game a physics based shooter that's actually kinda scary i love how the levels slowly ramp up in the scariness and i love how difficult the game can be but also satisfying all the different types of enemies have to be handled in a different way they're all so unique and memorable peak game

When I first looked into buying crashland, I thought it was just another uninspired arcade shooter clone, but boy was I wrong!

I’ve never had an experience quite like playing crashland as it makes me feel things I’ve never felt in VR before! Mainly being the fear, the tingle you get down your back telling you to turn around and check nothing is there, and I get this feeling a lot while playing!

When I was looking on the store, I wanted a really imaginative game with huge space colonies and factions galore, but when I first played crashland, I realised that wasn’t what I was looking for at all! All I needed was just a nice, fun and difficult game to grind out and crashland does a really good job at this!

Gameplay elements such as the gunplay and gore feel great! The progression also makes you keep coming back to try and level up some more and unlock new perks!

The only criticism I do have about this game is that:

A) There is no other guns beside the pistol and boomstick. Seeing some other kind of weapon or maybe even just a weapon skin system would be cool.

B) No online play. I know this would be costly to implement and run, but it would make crashland so much more fun!

C) I absolutely wish there was a map builder. I’m not asking for some fully-fledged state of the art premium nonsense, instead something along the lines of which map you want to choose, how long till’ salvation, what power-ups there will be and the enemy group selection.

All that being said, crashland is a great game. If you’re skeptical of this game looking at it, thinking “Well i’m not sure.” I can guarantee to you that if you buy it, you will be having fun for months!

This one is definitely a hidden gem, I've had my quest 3 for well over a year and somehow missed this. It is definitely a challenge and can be frustrating but in a good way as it's really rewarding to complete a round. The game dynamics and graphics are really well done. Well done devs!

reviews.review_title: This game sucks because of gun haters
reviews.review_description: Any person who designs a game where you have to be reliant on a pistol has never, ever had to fight a pack of wild feral dogs with just a pistol.  In this game, they expect you to survive with limited transportability and a pistol where you have to shoot ticks that are heavily armored in suck, you dry before you can even have a hope of killing them.Because there's not one , but there's always two or three that are impossible to kill with a pistol. And you can only kill with a grenade, or exploding flute fruit that has A very small blast radius get the bugs are always able to run pass before you can make it explode. but you have no grenades for the first 3 minutes. But you can't ever get the grenades, because you always die. The minute you touch the landing pod, because you're getting attacked by spiders ticks that you can't kill unless you shoot them hundreds of times and go up against electric slugs. This Game is only for people who love to kill hundreds of times because you're under equipped

reviews.review_title: A game you can't help but go back to
reviews.review_description:
This game is hella fun and requires a good bit of strategy in order to pull off, teleporting is a prime example, it's your best method of escape from Hordes of enemies. And you have to use the grenades very smartly in order to efficiently kill the most not enemies. However sometimes you can and will find yourself in an unwinnable situation and that's what makes the game frustrating and will make you quit for a while then come back too it.
This isn't the most perfect game but it's a good game nonetheless

Edit: Came back to it and it's still very frustrating

Wow, version 2.0 took me by surprise. 12 new levels and underwater environments.
Stunning on quest 3 with the qgo app!


Great graphics, decent difficulty, awesome sound effect, nice animations, cool environments, level up system with perks, many levels... 
This game shows that you do not need a deep story or heavy rpg mechanics. All you need is to get down on that planet and clean up the mess. Quick action to get into and really hard to leave.
I hope this game sells a lot so that we eventually get more cool features. Maybe even collect parts for our ship and do some drive by's and carpet bombing on flying boss like creatures or something. Love it!
Just want to add one more thing and it is the amazing lighting effects from the turret on the dark levels. You hear it go "thump! thump! thump!", seeing tracer rounds and the muzzle flashes illuminating the battleground. Wow! It's like heaven! :D
I would not mind seeing this title on the regular rift as well with even beefier effects.

reviews.review_title: An absolute standout in VR rougelikes!
reviews.review_description: Keeping it short and sweet. Brutal difficulty, amazing gameplay, satisfying combat, meaty weapons. A ton of levels, a ton of enemies. The game is a steal for the price. Also, insane replay value! One more thing to highlight, it's the most accurate simulation of how it feels getting eaten! So, in case you wanted to experience that... ;)
Lots of monsters, looks good, plays good, very replayable. AN UPDATE after this long and they added a bunch of levels monsters and guns. THANK YOU. Best game on the quest.

I’ve been playing this game for a few years.   If you are new to the game,  there’s a few pointers I can give.
Also if you get into danger, you can use the teleport, ￼ but use it sparingly because you can run out of them temporarily.￼   Use the orange explosive pods scattered about to take out enemies.  There are perks you can use when you get going in the game.  The most useful ones I found were speedy and turret￼.  The graphics and the alien enemy design is just fantastic.  ￼ Stick with it and learn the mechanics and you’ll find this game is tough but rewarding.   If it gets too intense, you can always scale it back a difficulty level.  Starting out on the normal difficulty is pretty tough. You might wanna turn it down a notch to start.   This game is absolutely awesome! Love it!
Update: 12 new levels !!! Thank you!!!
Super cool -but challenging ; )

reviews.review_title: I think I’m on to something
reviews.review_description:
yk now that this game seems to be getting more features from devs I think I got a lil sum

Sandbox modes and player level creation:
Basically how this would work is sandbox mode and level creation for other users to play would be accessible by beating either all campaign or special missions. In the sandbox mode you will be able to spawn enemies with some kind of spawn panel, and there will be categories for what you are looking for. But the enemies would have to be unlocked by beating that certain mission they are in, whether it’s campaign or special missions. And you would also be able to make the enemies fight each other. And you will also have a admin panel for things like invincibility or turbo cooldown. You would be able to spawn interactive items like boost mortars, boom plants, and boost balls. You can also choose the map you want depending if you’ve beat that level or not. And for level creation you get to choose what enemies spawn and what kind of gun modifications the player has. This would be awesome if this was added, and I can’t wait to see what comes in the future

If you grew up watching Sci-Fi classic action horror like Aliens, The Thing, or even more recently Starship Troopers, then this is for YOU!!

This game may give you nightmares if you're not a fan of giant bugs, snakes, worms or any other slimy alien like creatures from your imagination.

It does tick all the boxes, and is worth every single dime.

reviews.review_title: One of the best out there
reviews.review_description:
After trying out hundreds of games on Meta Quest platform I have a quite high standards nowadays. I got this one after hearing about the new update(2.0) and I have to say that this kind of compination of content and quality is extremely rare among all the VR games today.

I was expecting a short rogue like experience, but instead this is more of open world based shooter where player is thrown into beautiful exotic planets filled with super well crafted creatures. Animations, graphics, AI, gameplay, tactical aspects... This one just nails them all. Bravo! Easily 5/5

reviews.review_title: A wave shooter done really well...
reviews.review_description:
My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.

reviews.review_title: This game made me act like a chimpanzee
reviews.review_description:
If you have arachnophobia Good luck 
this game is absolutely horrifying in a good way some of the creatures in the game are based off of real life bugs/animals there's disgusting worms and creatures that drop a organic version of the word war II bouncing Betty bomb 

There are also perks in the game. 10/10

(Edit) THEY HAVE DINOSAURS (pacifically terror birds)

(Edit2) At mission 9 it turns into a straight up horror game it's very dark and scary and you get a small ahh flashlight

(Edit3) There are more dinosaurs I only got to see the terror bird on edit two btw they added special missions and you go underwater and it's terrifying like subnautica especially this one deep sea creature it acts like a boost ball and when you get close it picks you up and kills you I think it gets impatient or if it gets hurt it stops pretending and also it flash bangs you for like 3 seconds it's absolutely terrifying it's like a xenomorph giant scorpion and a angler fish had a 3 way it's like a scorpion angler fish 

(Edit4) I forgot to mention The angler fish creature when you're not looking at it it gets closer so if you turn your back on it it would jump scare you

(Edit5) There's lore??? I noticed something about the deep sea map where you see the crab anglerfish There's deep points in the map where it switches to flash light mode because it goes deeper into the water and gets darker The further you go around those points there's another spaceship just like yours but with no lights on My theory is A person just like you crash landed in the ocean and try to survive but didn't but also met the angler fish creature I think the creature attacks other animals by imitating smaller prey so the medium size ones gets trapped and blinded by it and it realized humans go after boost balls for power-ups it learned that and started imitating them and communicating to other angler fish. But hey That's just a theory A GAME THEORY

reviews.review_title: Scared the heck out of me!
reviews.review_description:
Absolutely love this game.  Very challenging, very difficult, and very scary!  Sometimes things sneak up on you and start sucking the life out of you before you turn to see it!  This game is a MUST-HAVE!
UPDATE:  (one year later) This game still holds up to any shooter game there is out there, even the new ones.  Still GREAT to play, still challenging, and still heart-pumping!  
The only thing that could make this better is a multiplayer version.  Maybe 2 or more can play together at the same time.  My wife and I would love to take on these creatures together on some strange planet's surface.  
UPDATE:  It's been 2 years since my original post, and I still love coming back to play this game over and over.  I have finished it all the way through all 24+ levels and in all but the last difficulty level. 
UPDATE TO THE UPDATE: Five years I've been playing this game. 1/8/26 The new 2.0 update is FANTASTIC! The 12 new locations are so different than the first 24. INCLUDING A FEW THAT ARE UNDERWATER!  The 40+ new creatures are terrifying and challenging to kill.  This is one of the few games I have rated a perfect 10 and I own almost 800 games.
Cool game

Minor update:

I hope my initial niggle about movement doesn't put anyone off. Having had more time with the game it's ridiculously good and very addictive. Also, after putting the work in there is a perk that boosts movement speed a bit and it's just enough to make things feel that bit more natural.

Seems I was lucky to get this after a major update which includes additional levels and they are great. The underwater levels are exceptional - the look, the sound, the physics etc are all so impressive.

The overall variety of aliens and the variation that comes with different weapon pickups and eventually perks to match is fantastic. Highly recommend grabbing this one, and right now it's a ridiculous bargain in the holiday sale.

*****
So I've only had the game a short time but the main takeaway so far has been that while it is tough to play, the look and feel makes it worth playing, in large part due to the environment and creature design which gives off genuinely creepy alien vibes.

I have my niggles, nothing is perfect. The setup is straightforward, you stand in the middle of a wasteland and fight off waves of enemies. And I personally dislike the locomotion choice here. Your character moves very slowly and life-saving moves are reliant upon a teleport option. It quickly becomes overwhelming as a result.

I don't know why this is such a favourite in VR and why people feel the need to tie difficulty down to hobbling the player. Give me proper manoeuvrability and let me dodge around the enemies as I've been able to do in an FPS since they were made popular in the 90's. The game would still be tough.

Weapon wise it's mainly a pistol with an alternate gun that utilises pick up abilities. There are power pickups for the pistol but it could really do with a rework to make the energy build up more apparent.

Aside from that, I still love what I've played so far. For a relatively simple concept and one that's been out for a while it looks, sounds, feels great overall. I can see why it gets praised and wish I'd picked it up sooner. 

If you're looking for a challenging horde/wave shooter with a creepy vibe you should pick it up. And I hope the dev considers offering those changes in future and I don't know if it's something that would be considered but this would be great in co-op.

reviews.review_title: I had to leave another review
reviews.review_description: Had to leave another review after this new multi-level update. DEVs, you spoil us! This is one of my favorite games on the quest and with the quest games optimizer on the quest 3, your monsters have never looked more hideous! Current stuck on the Infestation level with those freaking spiders but I will survive and that victory will hit super hard just cause of how many times I’ve died lol that’s what this game does to you! Great job DEVs and thanks a bunch for the update. (Edit) 30min after this I finally beat that Infestation spider level pro tip GO AFTER THE WHITE SPIDERS IMMEDIATELY! I had to sweat for that one hahahaha

Nice one, super nice, sharp graphics (although i wished reflections and normal map/lightning wouldbe rendered stereoscopic)
Animations for the creatures and the creatures themselves are looking top notch, and the gameplay is challanging and fun. 
On the imperfect side- teleporting using a straight raycast does not work very well (if you point accidently too high it simply wont work) which can be pretty annoying in stressfull situations since this is crucial for survival. And havind any kind of "game over" event ( like a red screen and a scream or so)  instead of fading to black would also give it a more polished feel. Adding a bit of music could also underline the general arcade feel. 

Really like the tactical gameplay. As many others i do hope for coop mode eventually :)

5 solide stars from me (and i would wish beeing able to archive something close to that in unity)
Cheers

An atmospheric, quirky wave-shooter that succeeds on many levels and aspects. High quality and various enemy characters per level. Arachnophobes be seriously warned! (I kind of wish for an arachnophobia option, I still can't stomach Pale Spiders in the dark). Good selection of user options. Visually excellent in terms of information graphics in aspects such as gun and it's power, proximity detector, teleporter, and more.
It's very morish when you begin unlocking new perks and get the overall hang of it.
The initial oppressive difficulty has been turned down with new lower options. It has an initial steep difficulty until perks are accessible. Going by estimates of standards from most games: 'Casual' is beginners, 'Beginners' is actually Normal (and v difficult until you get 2 perks), 'Normal' is Challenging, with the highest being Hardcore. 
The free upgrade to Crash land 2.0 is excellent and proof v1 was no mere fluke of Game design . A great Christmas present from a great VR game.

This is the only wave shooter you will curse and swear at every difficulty level. 

I love the unique mechanics that are simple to understand, yet hard to master. 

I’m removing one star due to graphics and invisible walls - improve these and you have five!

Crashland has to be one of the most underrated games on the Quest store in my opinion. The combat is enjoyable, the designs of the creatures are unique, and the level designs are well thought out. There is also a decent amount of things to do, such as obtaining perks and playing on different difficulties. I don't see many people talk about this game which is a shame. So overall, I think Crashland is a hidden gem that deserves more recognition and praise.
Title says it all. It's a great game, lots of fun, but would be taken to another level if I could enjoy it with friends.

reviews.review_title: Game so peak I came back to an update
reviews.review_description: Even after years of purchase, I come by and notice 12 new levels and more monsters?! Insane come back, beat it all and it was absolute Cinema. Love this game so much please give it a go!

This game is literally what every vr experience should be.  Great graphics, completely immersing, and the ability to upgrade weapons and manipulate perks for bonus abilities.  Continuous action!  So fun , so challenging.   Guaranteed to love it.

Game runs flawless .



Ps. Very Difficult even on normal
Best game so far love this game

reviews.review_title: I don't even like wave shooters!
reviews.review_description: But this game just rocks!
Needs multiplayer. (great game)

reviews.review_title: There is one thing that this game needs.
reviews.review_description:
This game is a very fun, strategic arcade type game where you just click play and instantly load in after you die. Right now, there is just a floating screen in space that you click to select your missions and click start, and the spaceship that crashes flies beside you into the planet. Well, why don’t you make it so that instead of a floating screen, the screen is located inside the spaceship, and after you die you are inside the spaceship where you select your missions on a control panel. This makes so much sense and would add some much needed depth to the game 

TL,DR: let you be inside the spaceship instead of float in space to select your missions

reviews.review_title: Amazing and Fun SciFi Arcade Shooter!
reviews.review_description:
This is the alien monster VR game you have been looking for. Just a great single player multi level shooter with decent graphics, level ups, easy teleport and standard movement options.  Easy to play but really hard to win. Truly a VR classic worth owning. It's like those old Jurassic Park or Terminator arcade shooters, without the constant scene shifting, better graphics, and with non-stop alien monsters. 

The premise is simple. You land on one of several nicely designed basic planets (ie no buildings or anything complicated) and aliens approach from all sides. You have a radar, two guns, there are several temporary power ups to pick up during a game, and you can earn permanent power up abilities post each game as you rack up kills. The graphics are exactly like the demo video. Each monster has different weaknesses. The goal is to survive until you can be rescued. 

Note: there are a lot of complaints by people that they can't get out of training mode. Look to the bottom of the menu for dots. You can press the next dot to proceed with the next mission. The menus have a lot of options. Take some time to look around before quitting. I believe this was made by one person. Unbelievably fun!

reviews.review_title: How to progress from basic training
reviews.review_description: Just can’t get off basic training.   Every time I finish training and try to progress in the game it sends me back to the beginning training.

reviews.review_title: This is for everyone reading the 1 stars
reviews.review_description:
I’m going to adresse some 1 star review complaints in hopes that more people buy this masterpiece.

1. “Stuck in training room”

These people just don’t see the level selection under the main screen. It’s not very hard to switch levels at all

2. “No easy mode”

Simply false. It takes 3 seconds to go to settings and see a difficulty slider

3. “Too hard”

Games don’t have to be for everyone. If you can tell you aren’t the target audience, you shouldn’t leave a one star review. It unnecessarily hurts the game all while making you look like one of those grown adults that hate Pokémon games for being too easy. This isn’t meant to be a walk in the park. You are crashed on an Alien planet with dangerous creatures all around and little way of protecting yourself. The game does a great job at making you feel like this without being unfair. You need to learn how to position yourself and prioritize threats, learn which boomstick weapon deals with what, and learn when you got to pack your stuff and bail out of a bad spot. So put it simply, the game doesn’t hold your hand, you just need to get good.

For an update to the game that is already amazing.. hear me out.. how about voice activated weapon switch.. say hellfire activate hellfire.. say boomshot and  its boomshot. Got the idea from watching Judge Dread. I think if you did this minor update to the game it would make it so much better instead of cycling through the weapon modes manually. Hope you consider it, thank you.

This is hands down one of the best games for standalone VR. It's extremely immersive given the great graphics, procedural creature animation, intuitive controls and action packed gameplay. It gets the heart pumping FAST.

It's the type of game that makes you want to get good at it so it's definitely got some long-term replayability. You grow from scared and vulnerable to a certified professional bug blaster but largely due to increased skill as a player rather than the leveling system alone. 

The perk system is fantastic and subtle and allows for significantly different play-styles. 

The overall tone of the game is great fun. It goes from a kind of intensity but also a nice little bit of humour. The creatures and environments are amazing and unique and the weapons feel satisfying to shoot.

I can't say enough good things about this game!

Every time I go to play this game, I remember why I havnt played it in a while. How do you get out of training and save your progress? It’s not letting me out of the training. For years I’ve had this game. lol. What is this problem?

This is one of the all time greats in my book. The graphics are of the highest quality. The game has never glitched or given me any problems ever. This is one of the oldest games in my collection. I've owned this game for years and still play it. I can't say enough good things about CRASHLAND it's simply top of the line across the board.
Just an amazing game that will hardly be ever beaten by any other game in its category. Not sure why there is not a part 2 after over 4 years.
My main complaint is that the perk of an auto torrent doesn’t work for me. I keep firing in the air over and over and over and nothing comes down. otherwise this is a really solid game.

Right up my street, shoot anything that moves . I like my games simple but challenging. I don't do crafting, world building  or role-playing and this fits the bill perfectly.

So if sci fi based wave shooters are your thing (like me)  and you want a challenge then this game is the one !

It would have been amazing for a multiplayer co op where you can match up with random players from 1-4 and the rounds are harder depending on the amount of players this game would be amazing with company it’s way to scary to be alone lol my only friend is the turret in this insect filled world

the animations are very crisp and the physics are nice, but early game your shots are so unbelievably weak that you literally just have to die unfair deaths that you cant avoid over, and over, and over again,

there are no real strategies you can apply other than kill them faster than they can damage you or teleport under a hill to avoid fire

Mechanically this is a brilliant game. But why pay money to be frustrated? Even the developer warns it’s a difficult game. All the reviews are the same. “This is great, but it will piss you off. If you’re mad now, just wait until the next level.” I don’t play games to get frustrated. I have work for that. Spend your time and money elsewhere.
Please don’t purchase this game. You will stuck on tutorial.
Unable together out of training mode. Indicates training complete,then reverts back to training.

Crashlands is a showcase of what VR is capable of and even after all these years it still hasn't been replicated, much less surpassed.

It's a simple concept hoard/wave based survival until the timer runs out style of game which on paper doesn't sound that interesting.  
What makes crashlands different is this simple formulas quickly turns into a joyous nightmare of macabre creatures of all shapes and sizes from giant insects to raptor like birds to many others that I don't want to spoil.  All charging at you from various angles and vantage points, some fly, some burrow but all at some point in time will succeed in making you say "holy crap" out loud. Horror on flat screen doesn't scare me, horror in VR works a bit better but still doesn't scare me with the sole exception of crashlands which has given me more jumpscares then any other form of media full stop and I absolutely love it and I cannot stop playing, even after the credits roll I cannot help but crave another burst of adrenaline and I dive back in. 

This game is a masterpiece that stands the test of time. 

5/5

Hello-
I really want to enjoy the game, but I just can't.  Pulling the trigger over and over again with the right hand?.....

reviews.review_title: Great game which there was a coop option
reviews.review_description: Great game but it really needs to have a coop option to play with at least one other person. Back to back watching your partners back as he watches yours. There is definitely enough going on to be challenging for it to be a two person game !

So incredibly sorry. I wish i did not have to give this one star but it deserves it. No mater how many times i complete the training course it just returns back to the training course even after i have completed it. I have never once seen the game play. Not once. I must have completed the training course at leat 8 times. What a waist of time and money. I googled my problem and found that other people have the same issue. Sorry but this glitch is terrible.  One star.

Update. After shearching the internet I found a solution to the poor menu system the game has. If you toggle you to the right at the start screen it it will bring up your first mission. What needs to be on the start training screen is an arrow that points to the right that indicates there is a menu to the right. Sorry guys but pretty lausy interface.

reviews.review_title: No Transferable Game Progress To Q3S
reviews.review_description: My progress did not forward to Quest 3 S. I worked so hard for the progress, and it sucks to all hell!
You crash on a planet, shoot things, get rescued, rinse, repeat.  In 5 min you have played the enitire game.  Sorry, just not worth the price.

reviews.review_title: Meta made me keep this horrible game…
reviews.review_description: This is the least entertaining app I’ve ever tried.  Even though I requested a refund right away meta said I’d had too many returns (but won’t tell me what number is too many) and made me keep this hateful game.  Every now and then I play it, hoping I’ll enjoy it more once I get the hang of it… but I don’t get the hang of it or enjoy it more.  It’s boring repetitive and hard in a very unengaging way.  This is the most annoying game I can recall playing on any platform.  Give me my money back!
They send a million overpowered enemies at you at once and expect your marshmallow shooting pistol and grenade to kill them all.

Just awful.  I try to turn and it keeps teleporting me places I don’t want to go. Little skill. You just empty as many bullets as you can into bugs as they suck your life. Wanted to return it but I waited too long to try it out so I was past the 14 day mark because I certainly didn’t play it that long. Just chalk this up to a learning experience that I should try games as soon as I buy them.
Way to hard

Extremely pathetic and weak guns.
Your pistol is awful.
The grenade launcher is worse.
You have to wait for it to land to pick up.￼
Only one shot plus slow reload and no way to aim it makes it extremely useless.
power ups are just as bad.
the "chopper" looks like a fan and does nothing
most games will cheat with damage sponge enemies and too many enemies and this game is too unfair. Enemies can spawn anywhere at any time, behind and to your blind sides while distracted. Some are too fast and sneaky.
leveling up & perks dont matter.￼
30 perks but max perk is 5 to have at a time.
blood thirsty bugs are the worst, keeping you trapt, focusing on them while more bugs ambush you. The worst way to die being Completely helpless with very bad guns, no real power ups, the very little are rare drops. Open world is pointless when the entire game is against you. 
Lower the enemy number, give the player a fighting chance, ￼￼might as well be fighting with a stick due to how unprepared this rando pilot and gun slinger is. More guns, more power ups, more drops and better leveling up system to upgrade guns, health & armor.
The game has too many spider. Like six types
The web throwing spider can teleport in front of you. Worms head but you from a distance. 
Hard mode has instant kill.
The game randomly pauses and there's no resume button.... why￼
Why is bully 2 so far away?
Most of the perks dont sound good at all

I don't get it. I'm glad it was cheap, but a complete waste of money. I only played it for about a half hour after I downloaded it. I knew pretty quickly I didn't like it. Wish I had been able to request a refund.

Closest thing to a starship troopers vr game. The bugs are huge, gross, and make disgusting sounds. Adjustable difficulty for when u just want to relax and pop some freaks is really enjoyable. Having a great time playin this one.
How do you get out of training mode & play the actual game? This is frustrating. Refund needed

Update:

For anyone reading this review, Crashland 2.0 is already out in Open Beta!
To download it, simply click on the game version number and select the Open Beta version.

This version is absolutely fantastic! We have many more levels to unlock in addition to the ones we already know. The spirit of the game has remained exactly the same—the kind that makes you say “come on, just one last run!” and then you keep playing anyway because it’s exciting and leaves you with a great feeling.

A huge thank you to the developer for all the effort and dedication put into this. There are so many rough, half-baked games out there, while this one is polished down to the smallest detail—and that’s something I truly appreciate.


old review:

In version 1.016 i can finally play! I love this game but is too difficult, new version is perfetct in begimmers mode!

I do NOT get the negative reviews at ALL.
Follow the directions:
1) You need to teleport Often (each kill gives another teleport bar anyway), so Use it!
2) There are FOUR difficulty settings - i did comfortable 1st, to get the hang of it (because I'm not so good at Fps's yet)
3) The monsters & their animations (& sound FX) are AMAZING, as are the abundant worlds.

One guy made this game, and Massive props to him!
Thanks so much!

It took me hours to get through on Comfortable (because i suck :), & now I'm on level 21 in Beginner, with more difficulty levels to follow.
SO many hours of -yes- STRESSFUL fun!

Beautiful game, with mega-replayability.
(Also, the developer has a 20-minute walkthrough on Youtube, which i found Most helpful)
Thank you sir, for a killer gaming experience!
Definitely in my top 5.

Edit: This game ia fantastic. I am now at level 24 on Normal, & have had SOOOO much fun, over SOOOO many hours. This is the one & only native Quest game i keep coming back to. It sits squarely at my #1 spot.
BUY IT!
(Everyone who gave this one or 2 stars because they couldn't get out of training sucks & should be destroyed)
I tried refunding it months ago when i bought it, but i was denied. Oh well, but maybe i would play it in the future, no, havent touched it since.
Absolutely awesome game

reviews.review_title: Not what the trailer said it was
reviews.review_description:
It doesn’t mention when u spawn u use a pistol the entire time and another gun that doesn’t work unless u get perks off the ground which is just dumb it’s only single player and it’s the same thing every mission just different aliens like how bad of a pilot is this guy to be crashing all the time 
I want a refund bottom line
Really fun game. Definitely worth the money.

I was THIS close to giving 1 star because, as the one-star reviewers complain about, it doesn’t let you go past the training level. 
I was ready to give up on it and gave it one mire try and I’m glad I did. I finished the training and it brought up the training window again. 
Here you have to toggle to the right. It will bring up the 1st level. 
I love the game! Best thing out there!

Went thru the intro, and then on 'start' took me back to intro again without any other option did it a second time only to have it send me back a third after trying to actually begin the game. Very disappointed, wondering if it's possible to play?
I purchased this game and it won't let me out of training...:( what should I do.

I like shooters but this was just too much. It feels like you have to grind A LOT just to try to gain a little enhancement.

Even on the easiest setting, it was still way too much.
This game is a waste of money so don’t buy the game. It is boring and is way too hard for anyone to play it.

reviews.review_title: It's Rough but it'll work your reflexes.
reviews.review_description:
I'll say it this Game Kicked my Keister. There's very few instances where genuine accuracy is required but when you meet those instances you'll wish you could snap your aim like a Lock-on,

This game'll have you doin that alot as bulky monsters stance up infront of you or pin you in place as their friends try to sneak up on you from the other side so you gotta get real snappy on the weakness blastin.

It'll work out your arms Eyes and Mind to keep up with it all and leave you feelin like an action Hero when your done.

I'd give it 4 starts but Sandfish and the other burrowers stole the fourth star by bein annoyin lil buggers

Bought this game on about mid march,i completed the training level over and over and over again but for some strange reason that was the only level i can play even though it says training complete i wait but no choice to start another level and it wouldnt continue,after many uninstalls and reinstalls ive finaly managed to get it to load to the first proper level,its far far too hard,after many many attempt at trying to complete just the first level ive now given up,what idiot set this difficulty,AVOID AT ALL COST,read the real reviews lower down,they tell the true story,game is no fun at all,just the same play a bit then die,play a bit then die,play a bit then die,one of the worst games on the quest,so poor ive uninstalled the game without even completing the first proper level
This game is quite a challenge, but it doesn't feel unfair in any way. Requires you to be fast and make use of the equipment you have. Would love to see this with multiplayer.

I am about 5 hours into this game and I was in high hopes on this game and it let me down. There is always something behind you.  It never ends you turn around and something is behind you again over and over. Teleport and there will be something behind you and you never end up where you are teleporting too. You are tied down a lot also where you cannot move either a spider web or some bolld sucker has you.  3 minutes does not seem like a long time to survive until you try it on this planet.  So wish I had returned it.
A lot of fun, lots of replayability and "good" moments of frustration that result in triumph with enough effort.

Either nerf the aliens, buff the gun damage, or add easy mode. I can't imagine playing impossible mode, considering I haven't passed level one yet. Like I've read before, think about kids or people in general that have disabilities. Shouldn't we all deserve to see the end game if we all paid the same price?

reviews.review_title: DO NOT BUY IF YOU ARE LEFT HANDED
reviews.review_description: Game seems fine but if you are left handed then there is no option to change dominant hand or swap guns which pretty much excludes a good number of players. Additionally if you have a disability which means your right hand is affected then the same issue applies if you need to switch dominance to left hand. Not sure why such a simple thing is not a requirement for most if not all games on the Oculus Store.

reviews.review_title: This game is only right hand compatible
reviews.review_description: I’m a lefty and the game only lets you use the main gun in your right hand, totally ruining the game with this stupid design flaw.

I was level 14 and had won several missions when all the game data reset on me… just because I check the settings screen?? I admit the game would get 4 or 5 stars before but now I’m scared to play out of fear of another random data reset. Would be glad to change my review with some game support as well.
